meaning: adj.不安的,不沉着的,烦燥的,难以取悦的
examples: When you're sitting in the hot sun, fidgety and freaked out, do you really want to be lectured about the big stuff?
Mr Fukui is less fretful and fidgety.
It helps out when you're in a fidgety mood.
The fidgety defector, 20-year-old Ahmed al-Latouf, said he had served as an army mortar man.
Stone was his usual sedated self, but Farrell became increasingly fidgety throughout the interview.
